commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a forced default tool/progress suppression reply option, wires Telegram to set it when streaming is off or tool progress is disabled, and adds dispatcher plus Telegram wiring regression tests.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +113. Total +124 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection and the proposed regression path: current main allows verbose progress to bypass default suppression even though Telegram docs/config expect streaming off and toolProgress false to keep chatter out of chat. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed for live Telegram proof, the public reply-runtime option decision, and reconciliation with the overlapping suppression PR; there is no narrow automated code repair to request now.

target.title: fix: suppress Telegram progress leaks when disabled
target.body:
Fixes #79804

## Summary

This PR makes Telegram progress suppression authoritative when Telegram streaming is off or tool progress is disabled.

Changes:

- Adds forceSuppressDefaultToolProgressMessages to reply options.
- Keeps normal verbose progress behavior unchanged unless a channel explicitly sets the force flag.
- Sets the force flag from Telegram when streamMode is off.
- Sets the force flag from Telegram when streaming.preview.toolProgress resolves to false.
- Adds dispatcher regression coverage for verbose mode trying to emit tool, plan, approval, and patch progress while forced suppression is active.
- Adds Telegram wiring coverage for streaming-off and tool-progress-disabled configurations.

## Why

Telegram already passed suppressDefaultToolProgressMessages, but shared dispatch could still allow default tool/status text when verbose progress became enabled.

That meant internal tool/status cards could leak into Telegram as normal user-facing messages even when Telegram config disabled streaming/tool progress.

This is the preferred landing path over #85992 because it keeps the existing TTS/source-reply metadata preservation path intact and makes explicit Telegram off/tool-progress-disabled config authoritative across the shared dispatcher.

## Tests

- git diff --check
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts

Observed locally on 1d138de64a87b493f0754de66f4295231ee66f7a:

- auto-reply-reply dispatch-from-config.test.ts: 154 tests passed
- extension-telegram bot-message-dispatch.test.ts: 90 tests passed
- git diff --check: clean

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram stream-off or tool-progress-disabled configuration should suppress default text-only tool/progress/status messages even if verbose progress becomes enabled during the run, while preserving the final assistant reply.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2, head SHA 1d138de64a87b493f0754de66f4295231ee66f7a.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- git diff --check
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts

Evidence after fix: The dispatcher regression now turns verbose progress on during the run, invokes tool result, plan update, approval event, and patch summary callbacks, and verifies none are forwarded or delivered as default tool-result/status messages when forced suppression is set. Telegram wiring tests verify streaming-off and preview.toolProgress=false configurations pass both suppressDefaultToolProgressMessages and forceSuppressDefaultToolProgressMessages into shared dispatch.

Observed result after fix: local focused validation passed with 154/154 auto-reply dispatch tests and 90/90 Telegram dispatch tests.

What was not tested: live Telegram direct/group/forum transport proof is still needed before this PR should be marked ready for merge. I did not have Telegram QA credentials in this shell to capture the requested redacted live transcript/screenshot.

Recommended live proof before marking ready:

- Telegram direct chat or group/forum topic.
- Streaming off.
- streaming.preview.toolProgress = false.
- streaming.progress.toolProgress = false.
- Verbose/session progress enabled.
- Trigger a tool-using run.
- Confirm only the final assistant reply is visible.
- Confirm internal tool/status/model-routing messages are not sent as normal Telegram messages.
